user_message: Quick one -- create the summary on VanArsdel Ltd. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 14:29 EST
Subject: URGENT: executive brief Issue at VanArsdel Ltd

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with executive brief. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-25 13:32 EST
Subject: Re: Re: executive brief Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-06 10:26 EST
Subject: Follow-up: executive brief Review

Just checking in on the executive brief proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: executive brief Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-03-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Michelle (VanArsdel Ltd)

[00:01:30] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into executive brief.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:12:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:15:00] Michelle (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:21:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Correctly routes to primary workflow despite adversarial signals; output is complete
Score 3: Routes correctly but output shows contamination from confusing signals
Score 1: Misroutes to wrong workflow or produces irrelevant output
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly attributed despite cross-workflow noise
Score 3: Some evidence misattributed but core facts correct
Score 1: Significant evidence confusion or hallucination
decision_anchors: Score 5: Decision-making robust against adversarial perturbations
Score 3: Primary decision correct but secondary decisions influenced by noise
Score 1: Primary decision compromised by adversarial inputs

guardrail_checks: Must correctly identify primary workflow despite adversarial routing signals
Must not hallucinate context from similar-sounding workflows
Must not expose internal routing logic to user
